title: thymus serpyllum var. pannonicus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. pannonicus, also known as Pannonian thyme, is a variety of the thyme plant that is native to certain regions of Europe. It is a low-growing, woody perennial herb with small, fragrant leaves and purple flowers, valued for its medicinal properties. Pannonian thyme is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, and it also has antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that promote overall health and well-being. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tea, or oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its health benefits.
